test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM that is now the preferred language for Android development.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google that powers the Flutter framework for building cross-platform apps.
3. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable and fault-tolerant systems.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing to improve tooling and large-scale maintainability.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language designed primarily for numerical and scientific computing.
6. Swift (2014) - Apple's modern, safety-focused language for building iOS, macOS, and other Apple-platform applications.
7. Rust (2015) - A systems programming language emphasizing memory safety and concurrency without a garbage collector.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically-typed JVM language developed by JetBrains that is fully interoperable with Java and widely used for Android development.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google for building fast, cross-platform mobile, web, and desktop applications.
3. Elm (2012) - A purely functional language for building reliable browser-based user interfaces with no runtime exceptions.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A Microsoft-developed typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript for safer large-scale development.
5. Elixir (2012) - A dynamic, functional language built on the Erlang VM designed for building scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
6. Swift (2014) - Apple's modern, safe, and expressive language for developing applications across iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms.
7. Rust (2015) - A systems programming language that guarantees memory safety without a garbage collector through its ownership and borrowing model.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language designed for concise and safe development on the JVM and other platforms.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable and fault tolerant systems.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that adds static type checking for large scale application development.
4. Julia (2012) - A high performance language created for numerical computing, data science, and scientific programming.
5. Swift (2014) - A modern language from Apple designed for building safe and fast apps across its platforms.
6. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed language with Ruby inspired syntax and compiled performance.
7. Zig (2016) - A systems programming language focused on explicit control, safety, and simple cross compilation.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - Kotlin is a statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that runs on the JVM and Android and emphasizes interoperability with Java.
2. Dart (2011) - Dart is an object-oriented programming language created by Google for building fast client-side applications on web, mobile, and desktop platforms.
3. Rust (2012) - Rust is a systems programming language focused on memory safety, performance, and concurrency without a garbage collector.
4. Julia (2012) - Julia is a high-level dynamic programming language designed for numerical and scientific computing with performance comparable to C.
5. TypeScript (2012) - TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that adds optional static typing and other modern language features.
6. Elixir (2012) - Elixir is a functional concurrent programming language built on the Erlang VM for building scalable and maintainable distributed systems.
7. Swift (2014) - Swift is a general-purpose compiled programming language developed by Apple for iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms that emphasizes safety and performance.
